title: Odebrecht / Lava Jato — Argentina Corruption Map
summary: Between 2007 and 2014, Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht paid at least USD 35 million in bribes to Argentine government officials and intermediaries, obtaining approximately USD 278 million in benefits across at least three major infrastructure projects. The corruption scheme was exposed through Brazil's Lava Jato investigation and Odebrecht's December 2016 guilty plea in New York. Argentine judicial proceedings remain largely stalled, with only the AySA branch reaching oral trial stage as of 2025.

total_bribes_confessed.confession_venue: US District Court, Eastern District of New York
total_bribes_confessed.legal_basis: Odebrecht and Braskem guilty plea — violation of Foreign Corrupt Practices Act

sources.title: Odebrecht reconoció el pago de sobornos en Argentina por USD 35 millones
Odebrecht admitió que pagó u$s 35 millones en coimas en la Argentina
Odebrecht, la constructora más grande de Brasil, reconoció el pago de sobornos en la Argentina

projects.name: Soterramiento del Ferrocarril Sarmiento
projects.description: Underground burial of the Sarmiento commuter rail line in Buenos Aires. Awarded January 2008 to Consorcio Nuevo Sarmiento. Total investment ~USD 1,500 million. Work paralyzed, never completed.
projects.bribe_mechanism: Fictitious contract for 'technical assistance services' with a Spanish company. USD 10.3 million invoiced, of which USD 4.5 million were transferred to Andorra and used for bribery payments.
projects.fiscal_note: Government of Macri paid USD 759 million for this project between reactivation in 2016 and paralysis in 2018.
projects.judicial_status: Investigation stage — 50 businessmen, executives, former officials and advisors questioned. Supreme Court upheld use of Lava Jato evidence (Sept 2025). No convictions.

sources.title: Soterramiento del Sarmiento: cómo fue la maniobra de Iecsa y Odebrecht para desviar fondos y pagar coimas
Soterramiento del Sarmiento: fiscal ratificó coima de u$s4 millones
La Corte Suprema dejó firme el uso de pruebas del Lava Jato en la causa por el soterramiento del tren Sarmiento
Odebrecht en Argentina: demandas cruzadas y el caso del soterramiento del Sarmiento en un limbo

projects.name: Plantas Potabilizadoras AySA (Paraná de las Palmas + Berazategui)
projects.description: Two water treatment plants for Agua y Saneamientos Argentinos (AySA): the Paraná de las Palmas potabilization plant in Tigre and the Berazategui purification plant. Bidding process in 2007, suspected of manipulation.
projects.bribe_mechanism: AySA authorities manipulated bidding processes to benefit participating companies through illicit payments that resulted in disadvantageous contractual conditions for the state.
projects.judicial_status: Elevated to oral trial (2019). Oral debate scheduled to begin April 7, 2025. 290 witnesses, ~30 defendants. Judges: Enrique Méndez Signori, Fernando Canero, Germán Castelli. Prosecutor: Fabiana León.

sources.title: Juez procesó a De Vido, López y Baratta por cohecho pasivo — Procuraduría de Investigaciones Administrativas
Confirmaron los procesamientos por las coimas en Odebrecht-Aysa
Odebrecht pagó coimas por US$ 14 millones para ganar un contrato de AySA
Causa Odebrecht: la Corte Suprema dejó firme la actualización del embargo

projects.name: Ampliación de Gasoductos Troncales (TGN + TGS / GNEA)
projects.description: Expansion of trunk gas pipeline networks (north and south). The GNEA (Gasoducto del Nordeste Argentino) was designed to import Bolivian gas. Awarded with 77% overpricing. State spent USD 2,300 million (double original budget). Project abandoned in 2018 after a decade unfinished.
projects.overpricing_detail: Adjudication price USD 58/meter-inch vs. World Bank reference USD 35/meter-inch. Segment EPC8 in Chaco awarded 27% above official budget without justification.
projects.bribe_mechanism: Odebrecht contacted local intermediary Carlos Wagner who explained money was needed to pay bribes to officials. Private bidding called through Cammesa directed from the beginning to award to Odebrecht. Memorandum of understanding between company and Energy Secretariat before bidding.

bribe_records.note: These are only recorded payments from internal spreadsheets; total confessed bribes for this project were USD 25 million

projects.judicial_status: Investigation stage — split in two branches. Supreme Court rejected statute of limitations request (Sept 2025). Casación dismissed prescription in March 2026. Investigation remains open.

sources.title: Odebrecht confesó coimas de US$25 millones por los gasoductos

Lava Jato en la Argentina: constructoras con sobreprecios de US$ 300 millones
Caso Odebrecht: la investigación por coimas en gasoductos seguirá abierta
Procesaron a De Vido y Cameron por favorecer a Odebrecht en obras de dos gasoductos

persons_implicated.name: Julio De Vido
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Processed. Elevated to oral trial (AySA branch). Supreme Court rejected his appeals (Dec 2024).
persons_implicated.notes: Central figure — his ministry controlled all public works contracting during the Kirchner era.

persons_implicated.name: José Francisco López
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Processed. Convicted separately in 'bolsos del convento' case (USD 9 million found at convent, June 2016). Released on conditional freedom.
persons_implicated.bolsos_connection: Arrested June 14, 2016 at Monasterio Nuestra Señora de Fátima (General Rodríguez) with bags containing USD 9 million and a submachine gun. Prosecutors linked the origin of money to Odebrecht, though this was never formally proven in the Odebrecht case.

persons_implicated.name: Roberto Baratta
persons_implicated.role: Subsecretario de Coordinación, Ministerio de Planificación
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Processed. Elevated to oral trial (AySA branch). Arrested by Judge Bonadio in 2018.
persons_implicated.notes: De Vido's right-hand man. Accused by his own co-defendant López of being key bribe collector.

persons_implicated.name: Daniel Cameron
persons_implicated.charges: Favoring Odebrecht in bidding processes for gas pipeline expansion
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Processed. Negotiated private bidding through Cammesa directed to Odebrecht. Investigation ongoing.

persons_implicated.name: Ricardo Jaime
persons_implicated.charges: Called to indagatoria in Odebrecht case
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Called to indagatoria (2018). Already serving sentences for other corruption cases.

persons_implicated.name: Juan Pablo Schiavi
persons_implicated.charges: Called to indagatoria in Odebrecht case

persons_implicated.name: Gustavo Arribas
persons_implicated.role: Director de la AFI (Agencia Federal de Inteligencia)
persons_implicated.charges: Receipt of bribes from Odebrecht via Swiss bank account
persons_implicated.payment_detail: Five transfers in September 2013 to Credit Suisse account in Zurich from Hong Kong shell company RFY Import & Export Ltd, operated by Odebrecht financial operator Leonardo Meirelles. Transfers occurred one day after Sarmiento contract reactivation.
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Charges dismissed by Judge Bonadio (2018). Prosecutors appealed but superior declined to sustain. Effectively unpursued.

persons_implicated.name: Ángelo Calcaterra
persons_implicated.role: Accionista y directivo de IECSA S.A. Primo de Mauricio Macri.
persons_implicated.charges: Aggravated tax evasion, participation in bribery scheme via fictitious contracts
persons_implicated.judicial_status: Falta de mérito dictada (2019) — effectively cleared at investigation stage, though case continues against consortium.
persons_implicated.notes: Sold IECSA in 2013 before the scandal broke. Denied involvement. Prosecution requested his processing.

persons_implicated.name: Aldo Benito Roggio
persons_implicated.role: Empresario — Benito Roggio e Hijos S.A.
persons_implicated.charges: Participation in bribery scheme for AySA contracts

persons_implicated.name: Carlos Wagner
persons_implicated.role: Empresario — Esuco S.A. Ex presidente Cámara Argentina de la Construcción
persons_implicated.charges: Intermediary in bribery payments. Processed for AySA/Berazategui plant.
persons_implicated.notes: According to Odebrecht executives, Wagner told them money was needed to pay officials to win public contracts.

odebrecht_codenames.description: Odebrecht's 'Department of Bribes' (Departamento de Operaciones Estructuradas) used codenames for Argentine bribe recipients. The real identities have NOT been judicially established.

aliases.note: Highest-paid of the five — suggests high-ranking official

sources.title: Documentos de la 'Caja 2' de Odebrecht revelan nuevos pagos ocultos y 'codinomes'
Quiénes son los cinco 'guardianes' de las coimas que Odebrecht pagó en la Argentina

macri_2015.event: Fundraising dinner at Sociedad Rural, March 18, 2015 (ARS 50,000/plate)
macri_2015.note: Legal campaign donation, declared. Braskem later pled guilty in the US alongside Odebrecht.
macri_2015.other_candidates_mentioned: Odebrecht executives informally claimed to have also funded Scioli, Massa, and Stolbizer campaigns in 2015 — all three denied it and no records were found.

sources.title: Odebrecht aportó fondos para la campaña de Pro y otros partidos están bajo la lupa
Nuevo escándalo: Odebrecht donó $500.000 a campaña de Macri

lava_jato_scale_in_argentina.mechanism: Brazilian construction company partners with Argentine firms, wins bid with lowest price and BNDES financing, then inflates via 'price redeterminations' and contract amendments.
lava_jato_scale_in_argentina.bndes_role: Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES) provided financing that gave Brazilian companies competitive advantage in Argentine bidding, securing the most important public works contracts.
lava_jato_scale_in_argentina.oas_loan_example: In 2015, Cristina Fernández de Kirchner granted OAS (another Lava Jato company) a USD 964 million loan for Los Blancos hydroelectric project in Mendoza.

sources.title: Lava Jato en la Argentina: constructoras con sobreprecios de US$ 300 millones
Así se inflaron los precios de las obras de Odebrecht: de US$ 889 millones a US$ 3.000 millones

cases.name: Soterramiento del Sarmiento
cases.status: Investigation stage — limbo. 49 called to indagatoria in 2018, procedural situations undefined.

cases.name: Gasoductos (ampliación troncales / GNEA)
cases.status: Investigation stage — split in two branches. Casación rejected prescription March 2026. Still open.

systemic_obstacles: Lack of international judicial cooperation (Argentina never received complete Odebrecht plea bargain testimony)
Multiple appeals and procedural challenges by defendants to exclude Brazilian evidence
Fragmentation of cases across different courts
Odebrecht filed counter-suits against Argentina for breach of contract
No convictions to date in any Odebrecht-related case in Argentina (as of March 2026)

sources.title: Odebrecht en Argentina: un escándalo de corrupción estancado en la Justicia
Casación descartó la prescripción de la causa Odebrecht

connections_to_existing_entities.note: Cross-references to entities that may already exist in the finanzas-politicas investigation graph
political_figures: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner — President during the bribery period (2007-2015). Not directly charged in Odebrecht case but her government officials are the primary defendants.

Mauricio Macri — His cousin Ángelo Calcaterra controlled IECSA (30% of Sarmiento consortium). His intelligence chief Arribas received USD 600K from Odebrecht. His campaign received Braskem donation. His government paid USD 759M for the stalled Sarmiento project.
Néstor Kirchner — President 2003-2007. Odebrecht executives testified 'todo fluía hasta que murió Kirchner' (everything flowed until Kirchner died), suggesting direct involvement.
companies: Benito Roggio e Hijos S.A. — Argentine construction conglomerate
OAS S.A. — Brazilian construction (Lava Jato, Los Blancos hydroelectric)
state_entities: Cammesa (Compañía Administradora del Mercado Mayorista Eléctrico)
financial_channels: RFY Import & Export Ltd (Hong Kong shell — Meirelles/Odebrecht)
Banco Finansur (linked to López dollar origin investigation)

key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.name: Flavio Bento de Faria
key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.role: Director Superintendente de Odebrecht en Argentina
key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.status: Cooperating witness (delación premiada in Brazil). Ordered payments to the five codename recipients.

key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.name: Mauricio Couri Ribeiro
key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.role: Ex director de Odebrecht en Argentina
key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.status: Supreme Court rejected his appeals (Sept 2025). Facing charges in Sarmiento case.

key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.name: Leonardo Meirelles
key_odebrecht_executives_argentina.status: Cooperating witness. Provided documentation of thousands of transfers. Made the Arribas transfers from Hong Kong.
